text: I still have heart palpitations from that salt casserole. The wellness arc needs a warning label.
Something caught fire in the kitchen and everyone clapped like it was scheduled. I did not feel safe. I felt marketed to.
An heirloom did not survive my stay. Management called it rustic charm. My insurance company used different words.
I am still composing the formal complaint. The library acoustics are excellent for practicing outrage.
I asked if I could leave a one-star review mid-ritual. They said feedback unlocks after checkout. Bold policy.
Tonight felt too real. I have packed. I may also call my therapist and my lawyer, in that order.
Someone posted our ritual online without asking. Authentic occult retreat, my ass. I want my face blurred and my soul back.
Three of us compared notes in the hall and none of us agree on what we witnessed. I left with more questions than answers and I guess that's kind of good but it doesn't feel good.
We saw what looked like an old wine cellar full of 'precious bodily fluids.' That really weirded me out. I did research. Are they *serious* weirdos or just into Kubrick?
The food was the only normal thing about this place. I mean that as a compliment. I think.
Deeply weird in the best way. Already told two friends. One is booking. The other wrote a concered DM to my mom.
Something here felt genuinely old. I cannot prove it. I also cannot stop thinking about it.
The ritual was unsettling and excellent. I loved it. I am also leaving early because I need a secular breakfast.
No disasters, no miracles. A perfectly adequate cult retreat. The crystals were nice.
